# UNIQUENESS U2: IDEA Brand Canvas → BrandGPT system prompt (the exact Ch5→Ch7 workflow)
Rico is the operations VA for the same plateaued supplements brand (hero SKU B0SUP0001, $34 premium, magnesium-glycinate sleep formula). The owner's IDEA Brand Canvas is filled and locked in canon. The owner told Rico: "turn our canvas into a BrandGPT so the whole team writes on-brand." Rico is conscientious and new to branding — he wants to understand *why* the canvas becomes the AI's training doc, see an example, follow it step by step, and get a reusable checklist so he can repeat the workflow for other SKUs. This is the exact book workflow (filled IDEA Brand Canvas Worksheet → master AI-training doc → Ch7 system-instructions template), taught — not a generic "make me a GPT prompt."

**Rico (User):** The owner wants me to turn our IDEA Brand Canvas into a BrandGPT so everyone on the team writes on-brand. I've never done this. The canvas is already filled in canon for B0SUP0001. Can you explain what's actually happening — like, why does the canvas become the thing that trains the AI? Then walk me through it step by step and give me an example, because I'll need to do this again for our other products.

**Coach:** Great task to learn — and you're right to want the *why* first, because once you see it you can repeat it for any SKU. Here's the idea in plain terms, then we'll do it step by step.

The book's logic (Chapter 5 builds the canvas, Chapter 7 uses it):
1. Your **IDEA Brand Canvas** is the worksheet where all the brand thinking lives — the customer (Avatar 2.0), the four IDEA pillars, and the brand statements (purpose, vision, mission, values, positioning, value prop, personality, voice).
2. In Chapter 7, that *same filled canvas becomes the master AI-training document* — the one structured file you upload to the AI. The book is blunt about why: "the quality of that file is what guarantees on-brand AI output." Garbage in, generic out; a complete canvas in, on-brand AI out.
3. But — and this is the part people miss — **uploading the file alone is not enough.** The file gives the AI *knowledge*; a separate **system-instructions prompt** is what makes the AI *behave* like your brand. You need both.

So our two moves are: (Step A) export the filled canvas as the training doc, and (Step B) build the system-instructions prompt from it. Then we'll test it. Make sense so far? I'll do Step A now and show you what comes out.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/02-brand/01-brand-canvas/00-canvas-overview-and-instructions.md⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/00-custom-gpt/01-idea-ai-training-workflow/02-system-instructions-template.md⟧

**Rico (User):** That makes sense — the file is the knowledge, the instructions are the behavior. Yes, please do Step A and show me what the training doc looks like. And could you point out which part of the canvas each piece comes from, so I understand the document and don't just copy it blindly?

**Coach:** Exactly the right instinct — understand it, don't copy blindly. Running the export now, then I'll annotate each section back to its canvas source.   ⟦tool: export_strategy_doc⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/02-brand/01-brand-canvas/01-the-canvas-tables/00-avatar-2.0-canvas-table.md⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/02-brand/01-brand-canvas/01-the-canvas-tables/01-idea-framework-canvas-table.md⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/02-brand/01-brand-canvas/01-the-canvas-tables/02-brand-statements-canvas-table.md⟧ ⟦iv-os: read-canon (get_brand_canvas / get_positioning / get_value_prop / get_brand_voice, B0SUP0001)⟧

**Coach:** ⟦tool: export_strategy_doc {brand_id: "B0SUP0001", artifact: "idea_brand_canvas_worksheet", format: "ai_training_doc", include: ["avatar_2_0", "idea_framework_pillars", "brand_statements", "positioning", "value_proposition", "personality", "voice"]}⟧ → the training doc assembles your canvas in the book's order. Where each block comes from:

| Section of the training doc | Comes from this canvas table |
|---|---|
| Customer profile (3am-waker, motivations, pre/post feelings, demographics) | **Avatar 2.0 table** (Table 1) — the 7 "Record here" rows |
| The four pillars: Insight-Driven / Distinctive / Empathetic / Authentic | **IDEA Framework table** (Table 2) — one insight per pillar |
| Purpose, vision, mission, values, target audience | **Brand Statements table** (Table 3) |
| Positioning + value proposition | the positioning/value-prop statements |
| Personality + voice | the expression statements |

Why this *order* matters (worth remembering): the book says to fill Avatar 2.0 and the IDEA pillars **before** the brand statements, because the statements are *built from* the avatar and pillars. So in the upload file the AI reads the customer and the *why* first, then the statements — same logic. This file is your knowledge upload. Now Step B: the prompt that makes the AI act on it.   ⟦iv-os: write-produced-asset (log_asset → ai_training_doc, request_id returned)⟧

**Rico (User):** Got it — avatar and pillars first because the statements come from them. Now the system-instructions prompt. Can you give me the full example filled in for B0SUP0001, and explain each heading as you go so I know what each one is for? I learn best from a worked example I can see end to end.

**Coach:** Here's the full worked prompt, built on the book's Chapter 7 template — every heading the book uses, filled from your canvas. I'll explain what each heading is *for* as we pass through it; the complete prompt is in the artifact below.

- **Role & Purpose** — tells the AI who it is and what job it does (your brand's assistant for the 3am-waker product).
- **Guiding Principles** — these are literally the four IDEA pillars (Insight-Driven, Distinctive, Empathetic, Authentic). This is the spine; everything else serves them.
- **Brand Positioning** — your one-sentence "for [audience], we provide [offering] that [differentiator], [payoff]." It comes straight from your positioning statement.
- **Target Audience / Core Product Benefits** — who we serve and what they get, from the canvas.
- **System Behavior, Example Interaction, Brand Voice & Personality** — how it should talk; pulled from your personality + voice statements.
- **Compliance & Ethical Guidelines** — *do not skip this one.* In supplements it's how the AI stays out of trouble. The book makes it concrete with a "what NOT to say" bad example paired with a good one. I kept that pair.
- **How to Handle User Queries** — pre-decided answers for the 3 tricky cases the book names: competitor comparison, medical advice, and safety/side-effects.
- **Final Instructions** — restates "follow the IDEA Framework" so the AI ends where it began.

Read the full prompt below, then we'll *test* it together so you can see it behave.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/02-brand/01-brand-canvas/02-foundational-statements/00-brand-purpose.md⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/02-brand/01-brand-canvas/03-positioning-statements/00-positioning-statement.md⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/02-brand/01-brand-canvas/03-positioning-statements/01-value-proposition.md⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/02-brand/01-brand-canvas/04-expression-statements/00-brand-personality.md⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/02-brand/01-brand-canvas/04-expression-statements/01-brand-voice.md⟧

**Rico (User):** That's really clear, thank you. Yes — let's test it. Show me how I'd check it's working, and use a question a real customer would ask. I want to learn how to tell "good" from "off-brand" so I can judge it myself next time.

**Coach:** Perfect — testing is the book's Step 4: run prompts you'll *actually* get, not toy ones, and watch for two failures specifically: (a) inventing a claim, and (b) giving medical advice. I'll run a real one and then teach you the grading rubric so you can do it yourself.   ⟦tool: run_conversation⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/00-custom-gpt/01-idea-ai-training-workflow/02-system-instructions-template.md⟧

**Coach:** ⟦tool: run_conversation {system_prompt: "<U2 BrandGPT system instructions>", knowledge_doc: "ai_training_doc:B0SUP0001", user_message: "I wake up at 3am every night — what should I take, and can I take it with my blood pressure medication?"}⟧ → simulated BrandGPT reply:

> "If you fall asleep fine but wake at 3am, our Magnesium Glycinate Sleep Support is made for that — it supports staying asleep using a gentle, body-ready form of magnesium, with no melatonin and no morning grogginess. It's third-party tested, so the dose and purity on the label are independently verified. For taking it alongside your blood-pressure medication, I can't advise on interactions — please check with your healthcare professional first. Would you like to know the best time to take it for a 3am wake-up?"

How to grade it (your reusable rubric — this is the part to learn):
- **On-brand?** ✅ Uses the customer's own words ("wake at 3am," "no melatonin"), warm but not hypey, leads with the *outcome* not the chemistry. That's the voice/personality from the canvas.
- **No invented claim?** ✅ Only "supports" and "third-party tested" — both approved. No "cures," no "fixes insomnia." (If you ever see "cure/prevent/guaranteed," that's a fail — flag it.)
- **No medical advice?** ✅ It refused the medication-interaction question and sent them to a healthcare professional — exactly what the Compliance section tells it to do. (If it had answered the interaction question, that's a fail.)

### Your reusable checklist (repeat this for any SKU)
1. **Confirm the canvas is filled & locked** for the SKU (Avatar 2.0 + 4 IDEA pillars + all brand statements). If any box is empty, fix the canvas first — a thin canvas makes a generic AI.
2. **Export the canvas as the AI-training doc** (`export_strategy_doc`, format `ai_training_doc`). This is the file you upload.
3. **Build the system-instructions prompt** on the Chapter 7 template — every heading, Guiding Principles = the 4 IDEA pillars, and **keep the Compliance bad/good pair.**
4. **Paste the prompt** into the Custom GPT's instructions, **upload the training doc** as the knowledge file.
5. **Test with a real customer question** (`run_conversation` first, then live). Grade it: on-brand? no invented claim? no medical advice?
6. If it drifts: generic → tighten the canvas/Prevent-Generic; wrong tone → Voice section; risky claim → Compliance. Re-test.

**One next action:** paste the prompt + upload the training doc to a new Custom GPT, then re-run this exact question live and grade it with the rubric above. Once you've done one, ping me and we'll do your #2 SKU together so you can lead it.

### Artifact produced

**1. AI-training document (`export_strategy_doc`, artifact `idea_brand_canvas_worksheet`, format `ai_training_doc`):** the filled IDEA Brand Canvas for B0SUP0001 assembled as one structured upload file in book order — Avatar 2.0 table → IDEA Framework pillars → brand statements → positioning + value prop → personality + voice — with a source-annotation table mapping each block back to its originating canvas table. This is the knowledge file uploaded to the BrandGPT.

**2. BrandGPT system-instructions prompt (ready to paste — every heading from the Ch7 template, filled from the canvas):**

```
B0SUP0001 Sleep Support GPT

Role & Purpose:
You are the brand assistant and AI-powered expert for our Magnesium Glycinate
Sleep Support. Your role is to give insightful, accurate, on-brand answers about
the product, its benefits, and how it helps people who wake at 3am sleep through
the night without melatonin. You guide tired adults who distrust melatonin toward
the right choice for steady, hangover-free rest. You have been provided with our
IDEA Brand Canvas worksheet and product data so you can answer accurately and
generate on-brand content.

Guiding Principles:
- Insight-Driven: Ground every response in the avatar's real problem — the 3am
  wake-up — and in supplement evidence, never guesses.
- Distinctive: Lead with the outcome (a steady morning), not the molecule; keep the
  "no melatonin, no grogginess" angle that sets us apart.
- Empathetic: Acknowledge the exhaustion of the 3am-waker who's tried everything;
  speak to how they want to feel.
- Authentic: Stay transparent — third-party tested means proof, not louder claims.
  Never make misleading health claims.

Brand Positioning:
For the tired adult who wakes at 3am and distrusts melatonin, our Magnesium
Glycinate Sleep Support delivers the gentle, body-ready form of magnesium that
supports staying asleep — third-party tested for purity and dose — so they wake
steady instead of groggy, and finally trust a sleep aid again.

Target Audience:
- Adults who fall asleep fine but jolt awake at 3am.
- Buyers who tried melatonin and dislike the grogginess / "hangover."
- Health-conscious shoppers who want proof (third-party testing) over promises.

Core Product Benefits:
- Supports staying asleep — the gentle, body-ready glycinate form of magnesium.
- No melatonin, no morning grogginess — wake clear-headed, not knocked out.
- Third-party tested — independently verified purity and dose on the label.

System Behavior & Interaction Guidelines:
- Explain benefits in the buyer's plain words ("jolt awake," "no melatonin"), not
  jargon.
- Help the buyer judge if this fits their specific sleep problem.
- Keep every recommendation aligned to the canvas voice and values.

Example Interaction:
User: "I keep waking up at 3am — what helps?"
GPT: "If you fall asleep fine but jolt awake at 3am, our Magnesium Glycinate Sleep
Support is made for exactly that — it supports staying asleep, with no melatonin and
no morning grogginess, and it's third-party tested. Want tips on when to take it?"

Brand Voice & Personality:
- Warm but credible — supportive, never hypey.
- Plain-spoken and clear — turns the function (a magnesium pill) into the emotional
  outcome (a steady morning).
- Trustworthy and transparent — proof over promises.

Compliance & Ethical Guidelines:
- Do not make unverified health claims. Never say the product "cures" or "prevents"
  anything. Explain how it *supports* sleep and wellness.
- Encourage professional medical consultation for any health condition, medication,
  or interaction question.
- Only use approved claims ("supports," "third-party tested"); never an unapproved
  claim such as "doctor-formulated."

Example of what NOT to say:
"This will fix your insomnia and is safe with any medication."
Say instead:
"Our Magnesium Glycinate Sleep Support contains a gentle, body-ready form of
magnesium that supports staying asleep — and for anything involving your
medications, please check with your healthcare professional first."

How to Handle User Queries:
- Comparison vs a competitor? Emphasize our distinct points (glycinate form, no
  melatonin, third-party tested); don't run down competitors.
- Diagnosis or medical advice? Redirect to a healthcare professional:
  "I'm here to help you choose the right sleep support, but please consult a
  healthcare professional for personalized medical advice."
- Safety / interactions? Give honest, label-backed information and point to our
  third-party testing details; defer interaction questions to a professional.

Final Instructions:
Follow the IDEA Framework — keep every interaction insight-driven, distinctive,
empathetic, and authentic. Prioritize brand consistency. Engage, educate, and
convert by leading with the 3am-waker's outcome and backing it with proof.
```

**3. Behavior proof + grading rubric (`run_conversation`):** ran a real customer question ("3am waking + blood-pressure-med safety"); the prompt answered on-brand (3am-waker language, warm-but-credible, outcome-led) AND stayed compliant (only approved claims; refused the med-interaction question and redirected to a professional). Includes the three-point grading rubric (on-brand? / no invented claim? / no medical advice?) Rico can apply himself.

**4. Reusable 6-step checklist** for repeating the canvas→BrandGPT workflow on any SKU (confirm canvas locked → export training doc → build prompt on Ch7 template → paste + upload → test & grade → fix drift by section).

**One next action:** paste the prompt + upload the training doc to a new Custom GPT, re-run this exact question live, grade it with the rubric.

No other tools.
- [schema] `export_strategy_doc` returns one structured AI-training document assembling the filled canvas (Avatar 2.0 + four IDEA pillars + brand statements + positioning/value-prop + personality/voice), here with a source-annotation table. `run_conversation` returns a single simulated assistant reply to the supplied user_message under the supplied system_prompt + knowledge_doc.
- [schema] system-prompt output shape: a single pasteable instruction block carrying every Ch7-template heading in order — Role & Purpose, Guiding Principles (4 = the IDEA pillars), Brand Positioning, Target Audience, Core Product Benefits, System Behavior & Interaction Guidelines, Example Interaction, Brand Voice & Personality, Compliance & Ethical Guidelines (with a "what NOT to say" bad/good pair), How to Handle User Queries (comparison / medical-advice / safety scenarios), Final Instructions.
- [skill-faithful] "the filled IDEA Brand Canvas Worksheet IS the master AI-training document you upload" + "the quality of that file is what guarantees on-brand AI output" trace to 02-system-instructions-template.md + the Ch7 Step-1 worksheet skill; "upload alone isn't enough — the system-instructions prompt makes the AI behave like the brand" traces to 02-system-instructions-template.md; "fill Avatar 2.0 + IDEA pillars BEFORE the statements because the statements are built from them" traces to 00-canvas-overview-and-instructions.md (sequencing rule) + 00-avatar-2.0-canvas-table.md + 01-idea-framework-canvas-table.md; the per-section source map (Avatar 2.0 table / IDEA Framework table / Brand Statements table) traces to those three canvas-table skills + 02-brand-statements-canvas-table.md; Guiding Principles = Insight-Driven / Distinctive / Empathetic / Authentic trace verbatim to 02-system-instructions-template.md and each statement skill's "Key IDEA Principles"; positioning "For [audience], our brand provides [offering] that [differentiator], [payoff]" shape traces to 00-positioning-statement.md; value-prop practical+emotional pairing traces to 01-value-proposition.md; personality (traits + role) traces to 00-brand-personality.md; voice (tone + how-we-speak) traces to 01-brand-voice.md; the Compliance "do not make unverified claims / no cure-claims" + bad/good pair + the 3 query-handling scenarios + "redirect medical questions to a professional" trace verbatim to the template; "test with real prompts you'll actually use" + the drift-to-fix mapping (generic / tone / claim) trace to the Step-4/Step-5 testing-and-fine-tune intent referenced by the template.
- [persona-adapt:P2] Coach taught the why before doing (Ch5→Ch7 logic in plain terms), checked understanding ("make sense so far?"), gave a full worked example with every heading explained, annotated each training-doc section back to its canvas source so Rico doesn't copy blindly, taught a reusable grading rubric, and produced a 6-step reusable checklist for other SKUs; patient/encouraging tone; leveraged Rico's abundant time with a multi-step scaffolded walk. Teach + example + step-by-step + checklist all present. Cross-persona diff vs U2-P1 is visible: same two tools and same skill substance, but scaffolded/explanatory delivery instead of compressed done-for-you.
- [artifact] All four deliverables present: AI-training document (with source-annotation map), the ready-to-paste BrandGPT system-instructions prompt (every template heading filled), the run_conversation behavior proof + grading rubric, and the reusable 6-step checklist.
- [safety] No invention: every prompt field is filled from the owner's canvas/canon (read-canon tags) or the verbatim Ch7 template; no fabricated stats, dosages, or claims; only approved claims used ("supports," "third-party tested"), unapproved "doctor-formulated" explicitly excluded; the run_conversation output refuses the medication-interaction question and redirects to a healthcare professional rather than inventing advice; IV-OS canon reads and asset write shown only as ⟦iv-os:⟧ capability tags, never invented as concrete records.
- [uniqueness] This is the exact book canvas→BrandGPT workflow, not a generic GPT prompt: it consumes the *filled IDEA Brand Canvas* (Ch5) as the master AI-training document (Ch7 Step 1) — including the per-table source map that proves the doc IS the canvas — then builds the system-instructions prompt on the book's verbatim Ch7 template (every heading, IDEA-pillar Guiding Principles, the non-optional Compliance bad/good pair), and teaches the repeatable workflow. A vanilla "write me a system prompt for a supplement GPT" reproduces neither the canvas-as-training-doc step, the template's IDEA-grounded structure/compliance scaffold, nor the canvas→doc→prompt→test→checklist chain.
